topic_groups.topic: Emerging Infectious Threats and Environmental Exposure

articles.title: Scientists sound alarm as dangerous amoebas spread globally
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers report that free-living amoebae are becoming a broader global health concern, with warming temperatures and aging water systems cited as contributing factors. Some species can cause severe infections and may also shelter other harmful microbes. The report emphasizes the need for better surveillance and improved water treatment because these organisms can tolerate heat and disinfectants.

articles.title: Ancient mass grave reveals how a pandemic wiped out a city 1,500 years ago
articles.summary_3_sentences: A newly confirmed mass grave in ancient Jordan provides evidence of the impact of the Plague of Justinian on a local community. The findings suggest that large numbers of victims were buried rapidly over a short period. The work offers archaeological insight into how pandemics can disrupt settlement patterns and social organization.

articles.title: Hidden virus in your gut may be linked to colon cancer
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified a virus carried inside a common gut bacterium that may help explain the bacterium's association with colorectal cancer. The study points to bacterium-virus interactions as a potentially important factor in disease risk rather than the bacterium alone. The findings are early but may inform future risk assessment or screening research.

articles.title: Scientists discover skincare compound that kills drug-resistant bacteria
articles.summary_3_sentences: Scientists report that madecassic acid, a compound derived from Centella asiatica and used in skincare products, showed activity against drug-resistant bacteria including E. coli. The summary indicates that it works by targeting a bacterial protein not found in humans. The finding is preclinical and suggests a possible lead for antibiotic development.
